events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess 4-gas monitor readings: O2 20.9 %VOL, LEL 2%, H2S 40 ppm, CO 5 ppm. Determine if entry can be authorized for permit-required confined space. Need acceptable entry limits. OSHA 1910.146: acceptable entry conditions: O2 between 19.5% and 23.5%; flammable gas <10% LEL (unless hot work then <10%? Actually for entry, <10% LEL generally; some standards require <10% LEL); toxic gas below PEL/TLV/acceptable ceiling. H2S: OSHA ceiling 10 ppm, 8-hr TWA 10? Actually H2S PEL 10 ppm ceiling (general industry 1910.1000). 40 ppm exceeds 10 ppm ceiling, IDLH 100 ppm. CO: OSHA PEL 50 ppm TWA, 200 ppm ceiling? Actually 1910.1000 Table Z-1: CO PEL 50 ppm (TWA), 200 ppm ceiling? Wait Table Z-1: Carbon monoxide: 50 ppm; 200 ppm ceiling? Let's recall: Carbon monoxide: 50 parts of gas per million parts of air (0.005%). The ceiling is 200 ppm? Actually OSHA PEL for CO is 50 ppm TWA, and 200 ppm ceiling? Need verify. NIOSH REL 35 ppm TWA, 200 ppm ceiling. OSHA 1910.1000 Table Z-1: Carbon monoxide: 50 ppm. Some standards: 35 ppm TWA, 200 ppm ceiling. 5 ppm is acceptable. H2S 40 ppm is above acceptable. Also LEL 2% is below 10% acceptable. O2 20.9 normal. So fail due to H2S. Need decision fail, risk high? H2S 40 ppm is high (above ceiling, approaching IDLH 100 ppm). Could be high. Work_stage planned. percent_complete maybe 0? It's pre-entry test, planned not started. component_conditions empty. value maybe H2S reading 40. findings: H2S concentration 40 ppm exceeds OSHA 10 ppm ceiling/acceptable entry limit. actions: ventilate and retest, do not enter until H2S <10 ppm, investigate source, ensure PPE/rescue. references: OSHA 1910.146, 1910.1000. Need include all fields. JSON only. Ensure valid
